title: Comparison
subtitle: Comparing values
goal: Understand that a comparison always returns true or false.
explanation: Comparison means checking two values against each other.
JavaScript can test whether values are equal, different, greater, or less.
The result of a comparison is always a boolean: true or false.
If the condition is correct, the result is true.
If the condition is wrong, the result is false.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: A comparison is like a yes-or-no question. JavaScript looks at two values and answers true or false.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes expect a comparison to return the value itself. In reality, it returns only true or false.
